info.name: Rubick AI — Catalog Intelligence API
info.description:
# Rubick AI Postman Collection

Complete API collection for the Rubick AI multi-platform catalog intelligence engine.

## Prerequisites

1. Start services: `docker compose up --build`
2. Run migrations: `docker compose exec backend node scripts/migrate.js`
3. Seed data: `docker compose exec backend node scripts/seed.js`
4. Import **Rubick-AI-Local.postman_environment.json** and select it

## Recommended operation order

### 1. Setup & verify
- **Backend Health** → confirms Postgres + Redis + Crawler
- **ML Service Health** → confirms Python ML service

### 2. Browse catalog
- **List Products** → saves `product_id` automatically
- **Get Product by ID** → full product detail
- **Search Products (Nike)** → fuzzy search demo
- **Compare Products** → side-by-side (needs 2 IDs from list)

### 3. Price tracking
- **Latest Prices** → most recent price per platform
- **Price History** → 90-day chart data
- **Simulate Price Change** → triggers SSE live banner

### 4. Deduplication & enrichment
- **Dedup Check** → 6-stage pipeline demo
- **Get Dedup Mappings** → cross-platform links
- **Enrich Product** → rule-based + optional LLM

### 5. Crawler (real-time HTTP)
- **Crawler Status** → live jobs + HTTP stats
- **Test HTTP Fetch (Myntra)** → real price from website
- **Run Crawl Tick Now** → force one crawl cycle
- **Pause / Resume Crawler** → control background worker

### 6. ML service (direct)
- Call ML endpoints directly when debugging without backend proxy

### 7. Live streaming (SSE)
- Open `GET {{backend_url}}/api/stream/prices` in browser or use Postman's SSE support
- Then run **Simulate Price Change** or **Run Crawl Tick** to see events

## Service URLs

| Service | Default URL |
|---------|-------------|
| Backend | http://localhost:3001 |
| ML Service | http://localhost:8000 |
| Frontend | http://localhost:5173 |

item.name: 1. Setup & Health
item.description: Verify all services are running before testing other endpoints.

request.description:
Checks PostgreSQL, Redis, and crawler status.

**Expected:** `{ "status": "ok", "services": { "postgres": "healthy", "redis": "healthy", "crawler": "healthy" } }`
Direct health check on the Python FastAPI ML service.
Returns ML service name and version.

item.name: 2. Products
item.description: Catalog search, detail, and comparison APIs.

request.description:
Cursor-based product listing with optional search/filters.

**Auto-saves** first product ID to `{{product_id}}` for use in other requests.

Check `X-Cache` header: HIT or MISS.
Fuzzy search across product titles and brands.
Filter products by L1 category (Footwear, Electronics, Clothing, Beauty).

Full product detail including platforms, attributes, and prices.

Run **List Products** first to populate `{{product_id}}`.
Compare up to 5 products side-by-side across platforms.

item.name: 3. Prices
item.description: Price history, latest prices, and SSE trigger.

request.description: Most recent price per product/platform combination (up to 100 rows).
Time-series price data for charts on the Product Detail page.

Manually insert a price and publish to Redis SSE channel.

**Operation:** Open frontend (http://localhost:5173) — green live banner should update instantly.

Also works with SSE stream: `GET /api/stream/prices`

item.name: 4. Deduplication & Enrichment
item.description: Cross-platform dedup pipeline and attribute enrichment (proxied to ML service).

request.description:
Runs the 6-stage dedup pipeline:
1. Pre-filter → 2. Size normalise → 3. Title clean → 4. Fuzzy match → 5. Embedding → 6. Human review

**Expected:** `{ "is_match": true, "confidence": 0.85+, "method": "fuzzy", "stage_reached": 4 }`
Different brands — should reject at pre-filter stage.
Returns confirmed cross-platform product links from `product_mappings` table.

Extracts color, material, size, category via rule-based engine.

Falls back to GPT-4o-mini if `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set and rule coverage < 80%.

Updates product in database with enriched attributes.

item.name: 5. Crawler
item.description: Background HTTP crawler — real-time price fetching and monitoring.

request.description:
Live crawler state: jobs, platform metrics, HTTP fetch stats, recent events.

Check `metrics.httpStats`:
- `success` = real HTTP fetches
- `fallback` = simulated prices
- `failed` = HTTP errors

Fetches a **real price** from Myntra without writing to DB.

**Expected:** `{ "success": true, "result": { "price": 454, "method": "http_myntra" } }`

Amazon often blocks datacenter IPs.

**If blocked:** Set `SCRAPERAPI_KEY` in `.env` and restart backend.
Forces one immediate crawl cycle (picks random products, HTTP fetch + DB update + SSE push).
Stops background crawl ticks until resumed.
Resumes background crawling.

item.name: 6. ML Service (Direct)
item.description: Call Python ML service directly — useful when debugging without the Node.js proxy.

request.description: Same as backend `/api/dedup/check` but hits ML service directly.

Rule-based enrichment on raw product object.

Returns extracted color, material, size, category with confidence scores.

item.name: 7. Streaming (SSE)
item.description:
Server-Sent Events for live price updates.

Postman note: Use **Send and Download** or open in browser. Events arrive when prices change via crawler or simulate endpoint.

request.description:
Long-lived SSE connection. Each event:
```json
{
  "type": "PRICE_CHANGE",
  "productId": "...",
  "platform": "myntra",
  "newPrice": 749,
  "title": "Nike Dri-FIT Running T-Shirt Black",
  "source": "http_crawler",
  "fetchMethod": "http_myntra",
  "timestamp": "..."
}
```

**Test flow:**
1. Open this request (keep connection open)
2. In another tab, run **Simulate Price Change** or **Run Crawl Tick Now**
3. Watch events arrive in real time
